ld: The translation problem is better modeled as a resident governed language service than as site i18n or as a codegen-first machine language extension.
pr: Locale handling could be treated as a frontend content concern plus later backend hookup.
im: Canvas should define language kernel primitives and host adapters explicitly so web, native, widget, and agent surfaces bind to one control plane.

rec: Build the language subsystem as a resident service with catalog, policy, execution, validation, and render planes; treat current landing locale work as a bootstrap provider only.
fx: The product never expands beyond a small static marketing locale surface and does not accumulate shared semantic state.

rec: Constrain AI language execution on canonical surfaces to candidate selection and arrangement over approved catalogs until a stricter validated-generation policy exists.
fx: A future validated-generation framework proves safe enough to allow arbitrary model output on regulated canonical surfaces.

ld: An AI-native secure language is best modeled as an interaction-combinator graph-reduction substrate (HVM/Bend lineage): no global state/registers -> deterministic + reproducible + inherently parallel; the reduction trace IS the append-only tape; the program graph = the world-map/WSKG topology; AI is a bounded graph-coordinator (ADR-057 candidate-only), not a raw-bytecode emitter; security is a bytecode invariant (state-caging) not a bolt-on sandbox. Unifies broker/tape/capability into one substrate.
pr: A machine-first language = codegen with translation bolted on, or a frontend locale framework.
im: The language is downstream of everything Canvas shipped (schema-first ABI, broker, tape, capability, ADR-057) and would be cv-language's substrate.

ld: Three independent sources converge on one hard constraint: AI must coordinate APPROVED candidates, never emit raw runtime artifacts. (1) ADR-057 'AI does not own the words / coordination-only'; (2) 2026 generative-UI research: A2UI agents request from a pre-approved component catalog, raw HTML/CSS/JS is build-time-only; (3) the language-kernel artifact non-negotiable #2. Per PROTOCOL, independent convergence on a constraint is high-signal.
im: Candidate-only AI is a cross-domain invariant for the secure language: validated-generation is the gate that must clear before raw AI output is allowed on canonical surfaces.

rec: Spec the AI-native language as an EXPLORATORY graph-reduction substrate (interaction-combinator bytecode) with security as a bytecode invariant and AI bounded to approved-candidate coordination; keep it OUT of the build path until HVM-at-scale, state-caging reality, and web-vs-native substrate are grounded.
fx: Grounding shows interaction-combinator runtimes cannot meet the latency/security bar, OR a simpler human-syntax+sandbox stack dominates on cost.

stmt: Interaction-combinator bytecode + HVM graph-reduction (HigherOrderCo) give massively-parallel, pure, reproducible execution with no persistent registers/global state — the substrate's parallelism + auditability claim. State-caging (formal-verification-in-bytecode) is the security claim but rests on tertiary citations — aspirational, not proven.

2026 generative-UI consensus matches ADR-057: A2UI uses strict declarative JSON over a pre-approved component catalog; fully-generated raw HTML/CSS/JS is a security risk and build-time-only; frameworks use Zod schemas to register+validate components. Independent corroboration of the candidate-only / schema-validate-then-gate pattern.

PARTIAL grounding for the on-device curve (blindspot wm-bs-ondevice-consumer-curve): hybrid edge/cloud = Tier1 7-13B Q4 on RTX 5090 (32GB) 150-260 tok/s; Apple M4 Max 128GB unified beats discrete GPUs for small models; Whisper-large-v3 INT8 ~4GB 30-50ms; escalate to cloud 70B if query >50 tok; Firecracker microVM ~125ms boot <5MiB for agent isolation. NOTE: device specs, NOT a consumer $/token TCO — mitigates, does not close, the blindspot.

sm: Open standard for machine-readable AI agent communication, capability discovery, and constraint enforcement.

rec: Require every fleet agent to maintain an Agent Card (JSON metadata) and ingest Policy Cards (JSON Schema) for autonomous compliance (A2A Standard).
fx: AI agents achieve reliable cross-vendor interoperability without structured capability advertisements.
